title: Trump says ICE agents will assist TSA agents with airport security as DHS shutdown continues
description: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.
content:
US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents will be sent to airports to help with increasingly long lines at security checkpoints, the Trump administration has said.
US President Donald Trump posted on social media that ICE agents will go to ... [3281 chars]

title: Starmer to chair COBRA meeting over Iran war's impact on the economy, Sky News understands
description: The prime minister will chair a meeting on Monday to coordinate the government's response.
content:
Sir Keir Starmer is to chair a COBRA meeting on Monday, Sky News understands.
Ministers and officials will discuss the cost-of-living impacts of the ongoing Iran war and plans to help households.
COBRA meetings are the government's regular way to res... [2402 chars]

title: Dame Jenni Murray obituary
description: Fearless broadcaster who was the longest-serving presenter of BBC Radio 4’s Woman’s Hour
content:
Jenni Murray, who has died aged 75, was the longest-serving presenter of Woman’s Hour, bringing calm authority to the BBC Radio 4 weekday show for 33 years.
The programme that was launched in 1946 with cooking, cleaning tips and romantic serials had,... [8030 chars]

title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
description: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.
content:
Extremist Jewish settlers have carried out a spate of attacks on Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank, setting fire to homes, vehicles and agricultural fields.

title: Archbishop of Canterbury says six-day pilgrimage has been a joy
description: Dame Sarah Mullally will be enthroned at a ceremony in front of 2,000 people on Wednesday.
content:
The first female Archbishop of Canterbury said she was "very relieved" to complete her six-day walking pilgrimage ahead of her formal enthronement.
